Graduate School of Governance

Master of Sociology

A modern academic platform for the Master of Sociology study program focused on social transformation, cultural studies, critical sociology, community engagement, and research excellence.

Explore Curriculum Apply Now
Focus
4
Semesters
4
Labs
6
Mode
S2

Policy Studio

Evidence-based governance briefs, leadership simulations, and policy design labs.

Governance Impact Index

Leadership, policy analytics, and public value creation.

About Program

A graduate platform for public leadership and institutional transformation.

The Master of Public Administration program prepares professionals to design responsive institutions, lead public sector innovation, and translate rigorous research into policies that improve community wellbeing.

Governance

Institutional reform, accountability, public ethics, and collaborative administration.

Policy

Policy analysis, evaluation methods, agenda setting, and strategic implementation.

Innovation

Digital public service, citizen experience, agile bureaucracy, and impact measurement.

Vision & Mission

Shaping ethical, analytical, and adaptive public leaders.

Vision

To become a leading graduate program that advances democratic governance, public value, and policy excellence.

The program cultivates leaders who can read complexity clearly, serve communities responsibly, and build institutions that earn public trust.

01

Advance research excellence

Develop applied research that informs public policy and administrative reform.

02

Strengthen public leadership

Prepare ethical leaders with strategic, collaborative, and data-informed decision capability.

03

Innovate public service

Design services that are inclusive, measurable, accessible, and citizen-centered.

04

Build global perspective

Connect Indonesian public administration with comparative governance practices.

Why Choose This Program

A premium academic experience for professionals who move institutions forward.

Every learning component is designed to connect theory, policy analysis, field evidence, and leadership practice.

Modern governance lens

Explore institutional design, policy networks, public accountability, and agile bureaucracy.

Policy analytics culture

Use qualitative and quantitative tools to understand problems, evaluate options, and measure impact.

Public value orientation

Frame leadership around integrity, inclusion, service quality, and measurable social outcomes.

Research mentorship

Receive structured guidance from proposal development to publication-ready research outputs.

Professional network

Learn with public managers, policy practitioners, researchers, and civic innovation leaders.

Impact-driven portfolio

Graduate with policy briefs, evaluation reports, and a thesis aligned to real public challenges.

Curriculum

An integrated pathway from public theory to applied policy leadership.

The curriculum balances governance foundations, research design, policy analysis, leadership practice, and thesis development across four semesters.

Core

Public Administration Theory

Administrative thought, public value, bureaucracy, and institutional behavior.

Core

Governance and Public Ethics

Accountability, transparency, public integrity, and collaborative governance.

Method

Research Methodology

Research design, data quality, field inquiry, and academic writing.

Studio

Policy Problem Framing

Problem trees, stakeholder maps, policy narratives, and evidence briefing.

Core

Public Policy Analysis

Policy instruments, implementation, evaluation, and policy learning cycles.

Core

Strategic Public Leadership

Decision leadership, negotiation, crisis response, and organizational change.

Method

Quantitative Policy Methods

Statistics, survey design, impact indicators, and dashboard interpretation.

Studio

Public Service Innovation

Citizen journeys, digital services, prototyping, and service quality standards.

Elective

Regional Governance

Local autonomy, intergovernmental relations, fiscal governance, and public participation.

Elective

Digital Government

Data governance, smart public services, cyber policy, and digital inclusion.

Research

Thesis Proposal Seminar

Proposal defense, literature synthesis, methodology refinement, and ethics review.

Lab

Policy Evaluation Clinic

Evaluation design, field data, recommendation drafting, and public presentation.

Research

Thesis Writing

Supervised research completion, analysis, discussion, and academic editing.

Research

Publication Workshop

Journal targeting, manuscript structure, citation strategy, and peer review readiness.

Capstone

Leadership Portfolio

Professional portfolio, policy brief collection, and reflective leadership statement.

Defense

Master Thesis Defense

Final examination, research contribution, and policy implications presentation.

Lecturer Section

Mentored by scholars and practitioners in public administration.

The lecturer profile structure is ready for real faculty data, research portfolios, and academic links.

GA

Governance Analysis

Public Governance

Focus on accountability systems, institutional reform, and collaborative administration.

PA

Policy Analytics

Public Policy

Focus on policy design, evaluation, regulatory impact, and evidence-based decisions.

SL

Strategic Leadership

Public Leadership

Focus on change management, public ethics, crisis leadership, and negotiation.

DG

Digital Governance

Service Innovation

Focus on digital public services, data governance, civic technology, and inclusion.

Research & Publications

Research that translates public problems into practical policy insight.

Cluster 01

Governance Reform

Institutional performance, public accountability, civil service systems, and reform implementation.

Cluster 02

Public Policy Evaluation

Impact evaluation, program learning, regulatory design, and evidence-based recommendations.

Cluster 03

Digital Public Service

Digital transformation, data policy, service quality, inclusion, and user experience.

Cluster 04

Local Governance

Regional autonomy, public finance, participatory planning, and community resilience.

Student Activities

A lively academic environment for policy practice and leadership growth.

Students engage in seminars, field-based policy clinics, public sector case labs, research colloquia, and professional leadership forums.

Policy Innovation Lab

Students prototype service improvements and turn field evidence into policy briefs.

Research Colloquium

A regular forum for discussing thesis ideas, research progress, and publication strategy.

Public Leadership Forum

Professional exchange with public managers, civic organizations, and policy practitioners.

0

Research seminars per academic cycle

0

Applied governance studios

0

Coursework built around policy cases

0

Strategic study concentrations

Testimonials

Designed for professionals who want sharper public impact.

FAQ Accordion

Questions future public leaders usually ask.

The accordion behavior is built with lightweight vanilla JavaScript and accessible button states.

It is designed for public managers, civil servants, policy analysts, researchers, nonprofit leaders, and graduates who want advanced capability in governance and public policy.

Courses combine seminars, case discussion, policy labs, research clinics, leadership simulation, and independent thesis supervision.

Yes. Students are guided through research design, manuscript development, citation practice, and publication strategy connected to their thesis topic.

Applicants submit academic records, identity documents, motivation statement, and research interest. The admissions team can provide the current intake schedule.

Admission Section

Begin your graduate journey in public administration.

A focused admissions flow helps applicants prepare their academic documents, research direction, and professional goals with clarity.

Request Admission Guidance
01

Prepare documents

Academic transcript, diploma, identity documents, CV, and motivation statement.

02

Submit application

Complete the online form and select your intended study concentration.

03

Academic interview

Discuss your research interest, public sector experience, and graduate study goals.

04

Enrollment briefing

Receive orientation, academic calendar, tuition guidance, and thesis pathway overview.

Contact Section

Speak with the academic admissions team.

Use this contact surface for admission questions, curriculum consultation, research supervision interests, and partnership inquiries.

Email

admissions@s2-administrasi-publik.ac.id

Academic Office

Graduate Public Administration Office, Faculty of Social and Political Sciences
